mission.title: Install and Configure LiteLLM
mission.description: LiteLLM is a Python SDK and Proxy Server designed to facilitate interactions with over 100 LLM APIs in a unified OpenAI format. It is ideal for developers looking to integrate multiple LLM services with features like cost tracking and load balancing. Key trade-offs include the complexity of managing multiple API keys and potential latency introduced by the proxy server.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the necessary command-line tools to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

pip install litellm==1.82.dev2

```

> Install the LiteLLM SDK for Python.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Provision a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create litellm-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Replace 'us-central1' with your preferred region.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local environment to communicate with the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you are authenticated with Google Cloud.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for your LiteLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/main/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> This will enable autoscaling for your LiteLLM deployment.
> This will set up monitoring for your LiteLLM deployment.

steps.title: Cost Warning
steps.description:
Be aware of potential costs associated with running the cluster and services.

```bash

gcloud compute instances list

```

> Check for running instances and their associated costs.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LiteLLM
uninstall.description:
This will remove the LiteLLM deployment and all associated resources. Be cautious as this may lead to data loss.

```bash

kubectl delete deployment litellm-deployment

gcloud container clusters delete litellm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This removes the LiteLLM deployment.
> This deletes the entire Kubernetes cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LiteLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. After upgrading, verify that the deployment is functioning correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n litellm --output=jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/main/deploy/litellm-deployment-v1.82.dev2.yaml

kubectl get pods -n litellm

```

> Drain the pods before upgrading.
> Apply the new deployment configuration.
> Verify that the new pods are running.
commands: kubectl get pods -n litellm --output=jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to the API server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually indicates that the kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is down.

**Fix:**
Run 'gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region=us-central1' to reconfigure kubeconfig.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated to handle the current load.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the machine type in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Pods are in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the application inside the pod fails to start due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that Kubernetes cannot pull the specified container image.

**Fix:**
Verify that the image name and tag are correct and that the image is accessible.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new LLM APIs including Bedrock and improved cost tracking features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME STATUS ROLES AGE VERSION' with all nodes in 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and cloud access configured.
